scope.target: Repository-wide standalone assessment policy and Git identity contract
constraints: Keep assessments distinct from workflows and backlog items.
Preserve assessed Git revision separately from stable assessment identity.
Apply prospectively without relocating historical workflow reports.

steps: Define assessment ownership, paths, ID format, lifecycle, and immutability.
Define direct, assessment, and workflow routing.
Define branch and Git commit lookup behavior.
Define backlog and downstream reference behavior.
validation: Verify no workflow semantics are duplicated
execution.last_completed_step: Created and linked the canonical assessment policy across workflow gate, branch, commit, backlog, indexes, and bilingual root guidance.

results.summary: Defined standalone assessment mode, ASM identity and Git lookup, immutable report lifecycle, draft resume checkpoints, and workflow/backlog handoffs.
results.residual_risk: Concurrent unpublished branches can still allocate the same sequence; policy requires pre-publication reallocation and validation.
